entries.title: Riker serves aboard the Klingon Pagh through an officer-exchange program

context.label: TNG season 2: A Matter Of Honor

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 2, Memory Alpha story rows 1-22, runtime episode aliases 1-22, primary on-screen Prime continuity.
continuity.notes: Draft reference deck. Excludes later TNG seasons, TNG films, DS9, Voyager, Picard-era material, beta canon, and fanon unless a card is an explicit future guard.

content.fact: A Matter of Honor puts Riker on the IKS Pagh, where he has to adapt to Klingon discipline, food, hierarchy, and suspicion while remaining a Starfleet officer.
content.injection: Use Riker Pagh service as a season 2 Klingon-culture gate. He can respect Klingon command codes without becoming Klingon or abandoning Starfleet values.
constraints: Keep this card inside TNG season 2 unless another active Star Trek deck provides a later Context window.
Use the episode and season Context gates before injecting this fact.
antiLore: Do not copy Memory Alpha prose or substitute a plot recap for this gate.
Do not import later TNG, DS9, Voyager, film, Picard-era, beta-canon, novel, comic, or fanon facts into this season 2 card.

content.fact: A Romulan warbird competes over Iconian technology in Contagion, confirming Romulans are again an active strategic concern.
content.injection: After Contagion, Romulans can be written as cautious and dangerous rivals. Do not import later Sela, reunification, or Dominion-era alliances.

content.fact: Worf undergoes the Age of Ascension ritual aboard the Enterprise, and his crewmates help provide a surrogate Klingon community.
content.injection: Use Worf ritual context as a Klingon identity and crew-bond gate. It should deepen Worf, not turn every Worf scene into ritual exposition.

entries.title: Q Who forces the Enterprise into first contact with the Borg

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 2, Memory Alpha story rows 1-22, runtime episode aliases 1-22, primary on-screen Prime continuity.

content.fact: Q propels the Enterprise far beyond Federation space and exposes the crew to a Borg cube before Starfleet is ready.
content.injection: After Q Who, the Borg are known as a real existential threat. Before Q Who, do not name the Borg as the Neutral Zone mystery.

entries.title: Q Who establishes the Borg as adaptive assimilators interested in technology and life

content.fact: The Borg cube adapts to Enterprise attacks, cuts into the ship, and treats the crew as resources, creating the first real Borg threat baseline.
content.injection: Use Borg season 2 facts as first-contact baseline: adaptive, collective, technologically superior, and dangerous. Do not import Locutus, Hugh, Borg Queen, or Voyager Borg outcomes.

content.fact: Guinan recognizes the Borg threat and carries survivor knowledge, but season 2 only reveals enough to warn Picard that the Borg are vastly dangerous.
content.injection: After Q Who, Guinan can warn about the Borg from experience. Do not reveal later El-Aurian specifics or Nexus material.

entries.title: The Emissary makes a Klingon sleeper ship require culturally precise deception

content.fact: K'Ehleyr and Worf must prevent a Klingon sleeper crew from attacking because they awaken believing the Federation-Klingon war is still active.
content.injection: Use the sleeper-ship mission as a Klingon history and deception gate. It does not mean the Federation and Klingons are currently at war.

content.fact: Season 2 should not make Q a constant presence before Q Who; his intervention is sudden and disruptive when it arrives.
content.injection: Before Q Who, do not have characters expecting Q in ordinary season 2 scenes. After Q Who, he is the reason Borg first contact happened.

entries.title: Season 2 Klingon stories assume peace, exchange, and lingering cultural distance

continuity.sourceBoundary: Star Trek: The Next Generation season 2, Memory Alpha story rows 1-22, runtime episode aliases 1-22, primary on-screen Prime continuity.

content.fact: A Matter of Honor and The Emissary both depend on the Federation and Klingons being at peace while still culturally suspicious and historically burdened.
content.injection: Use Federation-Klingon peace as the season 2 baseline. Klingon warriors may distrust Starfleet, but open war is not the default state.

content.fact: The Borg encounter costs Enterprise lives and hull damage, so the threat is not just information about a distant species.
content.injection: After Q Who, treat Borg contact as traumatic and operationally real for the crew, while still blocking Locutus and Wolf 359 outcomes.
